zh.lifeSummary: Robert Pictavius de Poitevin於1132年出生在英國威爾斯。他和Mrs. Robert de Poitevin至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Robert Pictavius de Poitevin于1132年出生在英国威尔斯。他和Mrs. Robert de Poitevin至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Poitevin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Poitevin: habitational name for someone from Poitou, a former province in western France whose capital city was Poitiers. See also Poitevent , compare Potvin 1.

variants.name: Potvin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Armand, Gilles, Alphonse, Andre, Cecile, Michel, Pierre, Adelard, Adrien, Aime, Benoit.
French Canadian: altered form of French Poitevin (see Poitevint ). Compare Podvin 1 and Potwin .
In some cases possibly also French: variant (shortened form) of French Poitevin (see Poitevint , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Poitevent
meanings: History: This surname is listed along with its original form Poitevin in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors. The Huguenot ancestor was Antoine Poitevin from France, who immigrated with his family to Charleston, SC, in 1685. His descendants also bear the surname Poitevint.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Robert Pictavius de Poitevin was born in 1132, in Wales, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter with Mrs. Robert de Poitevin.

fr.lifeSummary: Robert Pictavius de Poitevin est né en 1132, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Mrs. Robert de Poitevin.

de.lifeSummary: Robert Pictavius de Poitevin wurde 1132 in Wales, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mit Mrs. Robert de Poitevin mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Robert Pictavius de Poitevin è nato il nel 1132 nel Galles, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Mrs. Robert de Poitevin.

pt.lifeSummary: Robert Pictavius de Poitevin nasceu em 1132, em Gales, United Kingdom. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Mrs. Robert de Poitevin.
